title: Life expectancy gap for Black Bostonians is growing, health officials warn
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Black life expectancy in Boston fell to 76.2 years in 2024, widening the gap to 6.6 years from 3.3 in 2013.
The Boston Public Health Commission's report cites unintentional overdose, cancer, and cardiometabolic disease as leading causes of premature deaths.
Boston invests $2 million in initiatives to improve Black men's health and form partnerships with Dana-Farber Cancer Institute.

title: Former Celtics center Luke Kornet calls out Hawks over ‘Magic City’ promotion
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Former Celtics player Luke Kornet criticized the Atlanta Hawks for their 'Magic City Night' promotion involving a popular strip club.
Kornet urged the Hawks and NBA to reconsider the promo, citing concerns over the objectification and mistreatment of women.
The promotion includes a performance by rapper T.I. and serving of Magic City's famous lemon pepper wings at State Farm Arena.

title: Married couple from R.I. identified as victims in fatal Swansea crash
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Carolyn Carcasi, 54, and James Carcasi, 53, were identified as victims of a fatal crash in Swansea, Massachusetts.
The crash at Route 136 and Route 6 involved a Subaru Ascent and Sousa's speeding Infiniti, resulting in catastrophic damage.
Driver Demitri Sousa, 28, allegedly involved in a prior Rhode Island shooting, is hospitalized with serious injuries after the crash.

title: Steve Kerr apologizes for comments about Kristaps Porzingis’ lingering illness
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Kristaps Porzingis, dealing with POTS, has played only 18 games this season with Atlanta Hawks and Golden State Warriors.
Warriors coach Steve Kerr apologized for discussing Kristaps Porzingis' medical condition, admitting it was beyond his expertise.
Porzingis, traded to the Warriors last month, scored 12 points in his sole game against the Celtics on February 19.

title: Taking stock of Bruins’ top trade chips entering the NHL deadline
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins are exploring trade options ahead of the March 6 NHL trade deadline, considering draft picks and NHL talent.
Key trade assets include Mason Lohrei, Andrew Peeke, Viktor Arvidsson, and valuable first-round draft picks from 2026 and 2027.
Potential trades may focus on acquiring players with term remaining, as the Bruins aim to enhance their playoff roster.

title: NFL insider: Maxx Crosby trade could happen ‘this week’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The New England Patriots are interested in Las Vegas Raiders' pass-rusher Maxx Crosby, who may be traded this week.
Albert Breer reports a potential trade, with other interested teams including Dallas, Chicago, Baltimore, and Philadelphia.
Crosby, 29, has drawn interest from multiple teams; Raiders' GM John Spytek anticipates he remains with the team.

title: Quincy man sentenced in fatal St. Patrick’s Day assault in downtown Boston
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Sanusi Sadiq, 31, was sentenced to 3-6 years for the fatal assault of Barry Whelan on St. Patrick's Day 2023.
Whelan succumbed to a traumatic brain injury after being struck by Sadiq during a racially charged altercation in Boston.
Sadiq was found guilty of manslaughter in a January 2026 retrial; a previous trial in 2025 ended in mistrial.

title: Bill Simmons seems convinced that ‘all signs are pointing to’ this date for Jayson Tatum’s return
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Bill Simmons suggests Jayson Tatum may return Friday against the Mavericks at TD Garden.
Tatum has been ruled out for Tuesday's game against the Bucks due to achilles tendon repair.
Celtics have not set a timeline for Jayson Tatum's return, despite recent clearance for 5-on-5 practice.

title: Should teens be allowed to carry handguns in Massachusetts?
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: The Massachusetts Supreme Judicial Court is reviewing the legality of a state law limiting handgun licenses to those 21 and older.
The case, prompted by Mikai Thomson's 2021 arrest, challenges the age restriction as a violation of the Second Amendment.
Gun-rights advocates argue the age limit conflicts with the 2022 Supreme Court decision expanding public carry rights.

title: 5 takeaways from Don Sweeney’s Bruins press conference before trade deadline
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins GM Don Sweeney aims to improve the team ahead of the trade deadline without sacrificing long-term plans.
Sweeney stated the Bruins are unlikely to pursue rental players, focusing on sustainable team building instead.
Boston may target players with existing contracts, using plentiful draft picks and prospects for strategic trades.
